Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(7 comments)

I think there are still a couple of cases that are missing here.

It would also be useful if there was a sanity.sh test case for this.  Possibly something as simple as:

test_56z() { # LU-4824
        test_mkdir $DIR/$tdir
        touch $DIR/$tdir/$tfile
        $LFS find -type f $DIR/non_existent_dir $DIR/$tdir &&
                error "$LFS find did not return an error"
        local count=$($LFS find -type f $DIR/non_existent $DIR/$tdir | wc -l)
        [ $count == 2 ] || error "$LFS find did not continue after error"
}
run_one 56z "lfs find should continue after an error"

Patch Set 3:

Matt, any chance to update this patch so it can land in 2.6?

Patch Set 3:

(7 comments)

Thanks, Andreas.  I've been busy with other tasks, and I haven't worked on this in a while.  I've updated the patch locally, and I'm going to run some tests here before uploading.
Uploaded patch set 4.
Uploaded patch set 5.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Patch Set 6:

Andreas, thanks for suggesting a test.  That actually found another case where it's not handling things correctly - when multiple paths are specified on the command line and one fails, the others are not attempted.  I've updated the test to check for that case as well as the original (some file within a subtree returns an error).

This broke the {a,c,m}time and {g,u}id options.  Only mtime was found, and it was found by tests not designed for that.  Uploading a fix, but we should probably add some tests to get full coverage around all of the options to 'lfs find'.

parents.subject: LU-4524 ldlm: improve ldlm_lock_create() return value

commit.subject: LU-4824 utils: lfs find should continue after errors
commit.message:
LU-4824 utils: lfs find should continue after errors

'lfs find' stops as soon as it encounters an error, such as EACCES.
Change the behavior to match the regular find command: just note the
failure but continue on, returning a non-zero exit code at the end.

Signed-off-by: Matt Ezell <ezellma@ornl.gov>
Change-Id: I16ca35ec193e36134e7b93d91a20ff1d0778fe60
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9794
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
